vision_report.biome_observations: The transition from visible, low-light botanical specimens to a complete black-out (Current) suggests either a catastrophic failure of the lighting system or a total removal of the biome from the observation area.
vision_report.temporal_deltas: Earliest: Visible pots and soil. T-2: Significant light reduction. T-1: Minimal visibility. Current: Absolute darkness/zero signal.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has transitioned from a state of 'Neglected/Low-Light' to 'Non-Observable'. No living tissue can be confirmed in the final state.
vision_report.anomalies: The final image is a total black frame, indicating a failure in the monitoring environment or the removal of the subjects.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit began with a clear view of the four registered pots. Over the sequence, the lighting environment deteriorated rapidly. By the final observation, the visual field is entirely obscured, rendering the biome unmonitorable. I have performed a frame-by-frame verification; the loss of the white rabbit anchor in the final frame confirms the subjects are no longer within the camera's focal range or the environment has been compromised.
